---
title: Data Governance & Policy
teaching: 50
exercises: 20
---

# Essential Digital Skills Episode 3: Data Govenernance and policy

# Module Overview

- **Module:** DSDS-M3 – Data Governance & Policy  
- **Course:** Data Science and Digital Skills (DSDS)  
- **Audience:** Early Career Researchers (PhD, Postdoc)  
- **Duration:** ~50 minutes directed teaching (+20 minutes exercises)  

::: callout-outcomes

## Learning Outcomes

- Understand the principles of GDPR and lawful handling of personal/sensitive data  
- Recognise University of Nottingham policies on research ethics and research data management  
- Apply data classification and information security tiers to research datasets  
- Identify key compliance frameworks and shared responsibilities in research projects  
:::

<br>

::: callout-questions

## Questions

1. What are researchers’ responsibilities under GDPR and UK data protection law?  
2. When does a project require research ethics approval at UoN?  
3. How should datasets be classified to align with institutional information security policy?  
4. How do institutional and funder policies intersect to shape digital compliance?  
:::

## Structure & Agenda

1. Data Protection and Legal Frameworks (~12 min)  
2. Research Ethics and Institutional Conduct (~12 min)  
3. Data Classification and Information Security (~12 min)  
4. Policy and Digital Compliance Landscape (~14 min)  

---

# Data Protection and Legal Frameworks

Data protection is a central pillar of digital research practice. Understanding the **legal environment** helps researchers plan responsibly, protect participants, and avoid compliance breaches. UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018 apply whenever personal or special category data are collected, processed, or shared.

## Lawful Basis for Research

- Six lawful bases under Article 6 UK GDPR  
- Universities often rely on **public task** or **legitimate interests**  
- Consent may be used but must be **freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous**  

## Special Category Data

- Defined in Article 9 (e.g. health, ethnicity, political opinions)  
- Requires both a **lawful basis** (Art 6) and an **Article 9 condition**  
- Common research condition: *scientific research purposes with safeguards*  

## Research Provisions and Safeguards

- Exemptions for research (e.g. purpose/storage limitation) apply only with safeguards  
- Safeguards: data minimisation, pseudonymisation, secure storage, restricted access  
- High-risk processing requires **Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA)**  

#### Task 1: Scenario Ranking (10 min)

::: panel-tabset
##### Question
Rank fictional data collection scenarios from **lowest** to **highest** risk.

##### Exercise

<details>
<summary><strong>Template</strong></summary>

| Scenario | Personal data? | Special category? | Lawful basis (Art 6) | Art 9 condition (if any) | DPIA needed? | Safeguards | Classification | Decision & rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| B |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| C |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| D |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Scenarios</strong></summary>

- **A. Public social media corpus**: Downloading MPs’ tweets for sentiment analysis.  
- **B. Staff wellbeing survey**: Anonymous design, includes health/ethnicity questions with explicit consent.  
- **C. Clinical dataset link**: Pseudonymised NHS lab results linked with demographics under a sharing agreement.  
- **D. Refugee interviews**: Immigration, religion, trauma discussed in interviews with interpreters.  

</details>

##### Follow-up
- Identify lawful basis and Article 9 condition.  
- Note safeguards (minimisation, pseudonymisation, encryption, retention controls).  
:::

---

# Research Ethics and Institutional Conduct

Ethical review ensures that research is conducted with **integrity, transparency, and respect for participants**. UoN’s **Code of Research Conduct and Research Ethics** sets out mandatory standards and risk-based review routes.

## Ethics Principles

- Respect for autonomy, dignity, rights, and safety  
- Responsibility to avoid harm and manage risk  
- Integrity and transparency in design and reporting  

## Review Processes at UoN

- Ethics review required where projects involve human participants, personal data, vulnerable groups, or safety risks  
- Proportionate approach: low vs high-risk projects  
- Possible routes: **School/Faculty REC** or **NHS REC**  

## Researcher Responsibilities

- Prepare Participant Information Sheets and Consent Forms  
- Submit protocols and Data Management Plans  
- Maintain records and comply with conditions set by REC  

#### Task 2: Ethics Mapping (10 min)

::: panel-tabset
##### Question
Determine if your project requires ethics review and identify the appropriate route.

##### Exercise

<details>
<summary><strong>Template</strong></summary>

| Element | Notes |
|---|---|
| Title & summary |  |
| Participants & recruitment |  |
| Personal data involved? |  |
| Special category data? |  |
| Vulnerable groups? |  |
| Risk factors |  |
| Ethics route (School/Faculty REC, NHS REC) |  |
| Required documents |  |
| Data protection artefacts |  |
| External approvals |  |
| International transfers / TR&I due diligence |  |
| Storage & access |  |
| Retention & disposal |  |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Scenarios (use your own or adopt hypotheticals)</strong></summary>

- **Low-risk survey** with adults on study habits (no sensitive topics).  
- **Interviews** exploring experiences of ill health (special category data).  
- **Fieldwork** in a setting involving vulnerable groups.  
- **Secondary use** of existing clinical dataset under data sharing agreement.  

</details>

##### Follow-up
- List risks and mitigations; confirm whether a **DPIA** is required.  
- Identify approvals and responsible parties; outline record-keeping.  
:::

---

# Data Classification and Information Security

Different datasets carry different risks. **Classification** ensures appropriate handling, secure storage, and restricted access based on sensitivity. UoN has four levels: Public, Internal, Confidential, Highly Confidential.

## Information Categories

- **Public** – intended for release without restriction  
- **Internal** – limited to University community  
- **Confidential** – sensitive, requires access controls and monitoring  
- **Highly Confidential** – highly sensitive, strictly controlled  

## Applying the Framework

- Match classification to dataset content  
- Choose storage (OneDrive, SharePoint, research drives, secure clusters) accordingly  
- Control sharing with permissions and audit logging  

#### Task 3: Classification Exercise (10 min)

::: panel-tabset
##### Question
Classify each dataset and propose secure storage and access.

##### Exercise

<details>
<summary><strong>Template</strong></summary>

| Dataset | Classification | Storage | Access controls | Sharing method | Additional controls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 |  |  |  |  |  |
| 2 |  |  |  |  |  |
| 3 |  |  |  |  |  |
| 4 |  |  |  |  |  |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Scenarios</strong></summary>

1. **Anonymised survey data** on study habits.  
2. **Pseudonymised interviews** on workplace health experiences.  
3. **Physiological sensor data** with geolocation and timestamps.  
4. **Aggregate dataset** intended for open publication.  

</details>

##### Follow-up
- Justify classification and storage decisions.  
- Map controls to UoN-approved storage and access practices.  
:::

---

# Policy and Digital Compliance Landscape

Research operates in a complex environment of **institutional, national, and funder policies**. Awareness of this landscape ensures compliance and protects both researchers and participants.

## Institutional Policies

- **Code of Practice on Research Data Management** – requires DMPs, governs retention, sharing, repository use  
- **Trusted Research (UoN)** – due diligence for international collaborations and sensitive partnerships  

## National and Funder Expectations

- **UKRI Trusted Research & Innovation** – secure global partnerships, protect IP and people  
- **UKRI Open Research Data** policy – encourages sharing with proportionate safeguards  
- Data Protection reforms – stay updated via DPO and Research Integrity  

## Compliance in Practice

- Requires collaboration between PIs, project teams, and support units  
- Breach reporting pathways: notify IT Security / DPO promptly  
- International work may trigger export controls, due diligence, additional approvals  

#### Task 4: Policy Matching (10 min)

::: panel-tabset
##### Question
Match scenarios to relevant UoN or national policy.

##### Exercise

<details>
<summary><strong>Template</strong></summary>

| Scenario | Policy / guidance | Agreements | Roles (Controller/Processor/Joint) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A |  |  |  |  |
| B |  |  |  |  |
| C |  |  |  |  |
| D |  |  |  |  |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Policy Menu</strong></summary>

- UoN Code of Research Conduct and Research Ethics  
- UoN Code of Practice on Research Data Management  
- UoN Information Classification & Security Guidance  
- GDPR & Data Protection Act 2018  
- UKRI Trusted Research & Innovation  
- Contracts & IP (Data sharing, processing agreements, international transfers)  

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Scenarios</strong></summary>

- **A. Export to collaborator abroad** using a cloud platform.  
- **B. Hosting special category data** for multi-site analysis with external clinicians.  
- **C. Publishing derived dataset** and code openly.  
- **D. Outsourcing transcription** of sensitive interviews to a vendor.  

</details>

##### Follow-up
- Identify required agreements and role allocations.  
- Confirm international transfer safeguards (IDTA/SCCs).  
:::

---

# Further Information

## UoN Training Resources

- [Code of Research Conduct and Research Ethics](https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/research/ethics-and-integrity/code-of-research-conduct-and-research-ethics.aspx)  
- [Code of Practice on Research Data Management](https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/library/research/research-data-management/code-of-practice.aspx)  
- [Information Security and Classification Guidance](https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/it-services/security)  
- Annual Data Protection and Information Security Awareness (mandatory)  


::: callout-keypoints

## Keypoints

- GDPR requires lawful basis and safeguards for personal/special category data  
- UoN ethics approval processes ensure integrity and accountability  
- Data must be classified and secured according to institutional policy  
- Compliance involves institutional, team, and individual responsibility  
:::

<br>

::: callout-hints

## Hints

- When in doubt, consult the **Research Ethics & Integrity** team or the **Data Protection Officer**  
- Build compliance tasks into project planning from the outset  
- Record decisions in your Data Management Plan  
- International collaboration may trigger Trusted Research checks  
:::
